what_it_does: Redefines client_id() and is_design_client() to resolve identity from the OAuth token's native server-set top-level client_id (matched against clients.oauth_client_id for the active claude-design row) instead of from hook-stamped app_metadata claims, taking the Custom Access Token Hook out of the claims path while keeping the existing 0006 design_client policies working unchanged.
observations: Header titled GO NATIVE, motivated by zero blast radius on the 16 live OAuth connectors on the shared project.
client_id() now selects clients.id where oauth_client_id = auth.jwt()->>'client_id' and status='active' limit 1 — returns the ROW id (e.g. 'claude-design'), not the native client id.
is_design_client() now checks existence of an active client row whose oauth_client_id matches the native client_id, replacing the 0006 app_metadata.role check.
Both functions are SECURITY DEFINER with set search_path=public and re-asserted via explicit ALTER statements; both carry descriptive comments.
Header documents the key mapping: native client_id is the Vi OAuth CLIENT id; RLS scoping uses the client ROW id; the Edge Function mirrors this lookup.
channel_granted() is noted as unchanged (made SECURITY DEFINER in 0009) and picks up the new client_id() automatically.
Header notes the boundary principal (0005) still reads app_metadata.role set at user creation — a clean split: CD=native client_id, boundary=app_metadata-at-creation.
standouts: The pivot from hook-stamped app_metadata claims to the native, server-set, non-forgeable OAuth client_id as the identity source — and the explicit ROW-id-vs-native-client-id mapping it depends on.
conventions: create or replace + explicit ALTER to re-assert SECURITY DEFINER/search_path (same pattern as 0006).
Function comments documenting semantics and the empty-string fallback.
Leaves downstream 0006 policies untouched by only changing the helper functions they reference.
notes: This is the 'go native' switch: identity now derives from the verified OAuth client_id mapped to a clients row, so the hook becomes inert for claims while the 0006 policies keep gating unchanged.
questions: Is auth.jwt()->>'client_id' reliably present and server-set for all the OAuth connectors on this shared project?
After this migration, is the 0007 hook fully removed from the active-hook slot, or only inert for claims while still staged for aud-binding?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: header says identity moves to the native client_id with no change to channel_granted or the 0006 policies, and the two redefined functions do exactly that.
apparent_themes: Zero blast radius on a shared multi-connector project
Clean separation of connector vs boundary identity
intention_signals: Trying to derive the design_client identity from the OAuth server's verified client_id rather than a custom hook, so the shared project's other connectors are untouched and identity cannot be forged via metadata.
novelty: The ROW-id-vs-native-client-id indirection — RLS scoping uses the clients row id resolved from the native client id, with the Edge Function mirroring the same lookup.
contribution: Contributes the native-client-id identity resolution for client_id()/is_design_client(), mapping a verified OAuth client to a clients row id without any token-mutating hook.
summary_for_embedding: Supabase migration redefining client_id() and is_design_client() to resolve the design_client identity from the OAuth token's native server-set client_id mapped to clients.oauth_client_id, removing the hook from the claims path.
intention_for_embedding: To base the design connector's RLS identity on a verified, non-forgeable native OAuth client_id with zero blast radius on the other connectors of a shared Supabase project.
